sources.notes: Combinatorial DP problem about attractions and valid arrangements.
Official tutorial derives a DP over compressed counts/classes with combinatorial coefficients.

annotation.summary: Count valid attraction configurations/arrangements under the statement constraints.
annotation.constraints: The total size allows polynomial DP over compressed counts, but not direct enumeration of arrangements.
annotation.core_idea: Group indistinguishable choices into classes and build the arrangement step by step. The number of ways to insert/merge a new class depends only on the current compressed state, so dynamic programming with precomputed combinatorial coefficients counts all valid configurations.
annotation.complexity: O(n^2) to O(n^3) depending on coefficient precomputation.
tricks: DP over number of processed objects/classes

solution_variants.name: compressed-combinatorial-dp
solution_variants.summary: Use DP over processed classes and multiply by binomial-style insertion counts.

tags.evidence: The main solution counts configurations by dynamic programming over compressed states.
Transitions are weighted by combinatorial insertion/selection coefficients.
